response_body.response: need to know your skin type first. is it oily, dry, combination, or sensitive. that determines which actives and how aggressive you go. once you tell me that, i'll map out the exact routine and products.

start with the cerave cleanser, niacinamide serum, and a lightweight moisturizer (cerave pm lotion works for oily skin). add the glycolic acid 2x per week in the evening.

consistency matters more than fancy products. stick with this for 4-6 weeks before adding anything else. texture improves when you exfoliate regularly and keep the barrier intact with a basic moisturizer.

products.name: CeraVe Foaming Facial Cleanser
products.description: Foaming yet non-stripping — for oily/acne-prone skin types.

products.name: The Ordinary Niacinamide 10% + Zinc 1%
products.description: 10% niacinamide for sebum control and pore appearance.

products.name: The Ordinary Glycolic Acid 7% Toning Solution
products.description: 7% glycolic AHA toner — start 2x/week, do not combine with retinol.

products.name: CeraVe PM Facial Moisturizing Lotion
products.description: Lightweight ceramide + niacinamide moisturizer for nightly use.

response_body.response: you've got oily skin, so you want a lightweight moisturizer with ceramides and panthenol that won't clog you. skip heavy creams. look for a gel-cream or lotion formula that says "ceramides + panthenol" on the label. apply it after your active (azelaic or whatever you're using) while your skin is still slightly damp to lock in hydration. specific brands aren't in your current docs. what moisturizer are you using now, or do you want me to pull product recs from another module.

products.name: The Ordinary Azelaic Acid Suspension 10%
products.description: Azelaic acid for redness, PIH, and mild rosacea — pregnancy-safe.

products.name: CeraVe PM Facial Moisturizing Lotion
products.description: Lightweight ceramide + niacinamide moisturizer for nightly use.
